document.source_title: 8 in 10 working Indians are overweight: Most are already sick and don’t know it, says survey

claims.evidence_text: In working populations (average age 38), 8 in 10 were overweight.

claims.claim_text: Nearly half of working Indians have prediabetes or diabetes.
claims.evidence_text: In working populations, nearly half have prediabetes or diabetes.

claims.claim_text: Health risks are present in young populations.
claims.evidence_text: Health risks are already present in young and working populations, even before symptoms appear.

entities.name: Apollo Hospitals
entities.description: Healthcare organization that released the report.
entities.evidence_text: according to the sixth edition of the Apollo Hospitals’ Health of the Nation 2026 report

entities.name: Dr Saptarshi Bhattacharya
entities.description: Senior endocrinologist at Indraprastha Apollo Hospital, New Delhi.
entities.evidence_text: says Dr Saptarshi Bhattacharya, senior endocrinologist, Indraprastha Apollo Hospital, New Delhi

metrics.name: Percentage of overweight working Indians
metrics.evidence_text: In working populations (average age 38), 8 in 10 were overweight.

metrics.name: Percentage of working Indians with prediabetes or diabetes
metrics.evidence_text: In working populations, nearly half have prediabetes or diabetes.

metrics.name: Percentage of under-30s with poor flexibility, strength or balance
metrics.evidence_text: Nearly two-thirds of under-30s assessed had poor flexibility, strength or balance.

citations.title: 8 in 10 working Indians are overweight: Most are already sick and don’t know it, says survey

source_credibility.reason: The report is based on over three million preventive health assessments conducted across the hospital ecosystem.
